primary_reason: This review addresses diagnosis and treatment of tumefactive demyelinating lesions without an infectious-disease outcome or stated transfer route.
This multicentre serosurvey uses pertussis-toxin antibodies to estimate infection incidence and quantify underreporting relative to notifications.
This structured survey measures provider knowledge, practices, and barriers affecting adverse-event surveillance after immunization.
This scoping review diagnoses numerator, denominator, access, and ascertainment biases affecting infectious-disease estimates among migrants.
This study evaluates a crowdsourced foodborne-illness platform as a supplement for traditional reporting and early outbreak detection.
This application uses repeated standardized point-prevalence surveys to measure healthcare-associated infections and their temporal changes.
This hospital comparison uses blood-culture utilization as a measurement of microbiological surveillance capacity and potential AMR underascertainment.
This study combines spatial-video geonarratives, mapped environmental risks, and case densities to identify arboviral hotspots and possible underreporting.
This imaging study concerns complex liver cysts in polycystic kidney disease and provides no infectious-disease transfer route.
This cohort actively traces MDR-TB patients recorded as lost to follow-up to correct treatment outcomes and reveal underreported success.
This population-based field study jointly measures tuberculosis, latent infection, intestinal parasites, contacts, and environmental conditions in a mobile community.
This vaccine-safety study combines case validation, capture-recapture adjustment, and observed-versus-expected analysis of post-vaccination narcolepsy.
This systematic review pools age-specific serology and applies catalytic force-of-infection models to derive varicella incidence without case notifications.
This survey describes reported sexual-partnership patterns and correlates without validating or correcting the acknowledged reporting bias.
This cross-national hemovigilance analysis compares septic-reaction rates under bacterial culture and pathogen-reduction strategies while assessing reporting limitations.
This dengue application models daily asymptomatic donor viremia from infection, symptom, viremia-duration, and reporting parameters to guide blood screening.
This systematic review identifies barriers to private-practitioner disease notification and prescribes training, feedback, supervision, and regulatory remedies.
This general life-expectancy analysis is dominated by noncommunicable mortality and does not specify an infectious-disease transfer route for its adjustment.
This simulation-based methods study evaluates surrogate-network reconstructions for correcting epidemic-risk estimates from non-uniformly sampled contact data.
This systematic review uses Bayesian bivariate random-effects models to compare the accuracy of three classes of rapid influenza tests.
This laboratory assessment shows that travel-based Giardia testing criteria miss most confirmed cases and recommends universal stool testing.
This individual-based Monte Carlo study evaluates nonpaternity and half-sibling ratios as objective proxies for underreported extramarital sex.
This audit classifies erroneous HIV-intervention records and their causes to identify data-quality controls for network reporting.
This review summarizes healthcare-worker knowledge and risk perceptions but does not develop or substantively compare a surveillance or measurement method.
This study manually compares two indigenous-health data systems to identify underreported gestational, congenital, and acquired syphilis.
This cluster-randomized trial evaluates a multicomponent hand-hygiene program using infection, mortality, hospitalization, and antibiotic-use outcomes.
This cross-sectional survey describes student nurses' needlestick knowledge, exposure, and reporting without a reusable correction or validation design.
This paper defines surveillance fatigue as epidemic-driven case underreporting and supports the diagnostic concept using historical epidemics.
This outbreak investigation retests dengue-negative specimens for chikungunya and adds virus isolation and phylogenetics to detect masked cocirculation.
This mixed-methods trypanosomiasis study combines active case tracing, interviews, focus groups, and disability-adjusted burden estimates.
This psychometric study evaluates and refines two ACASI-administered HIV-risk instruments for reliability, discrimination, and convergent validity.
This application uses ensemble random forests to map anthrax suitability and estimate livestock and human populations at risk.
This pregnancy energy-intake study addresses dietary misreporting without an infectious outcome or a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This HIV and HCV cohort combines self-reported alcohol use with a direct biomarker to detect exposure misclassification associated with mortality.
This decision-tree and Markov analysis evaluates maternal GBS vaccination across countries, underreporting assumptions, efficacy ranges, and cost-effectiveness thresholds.
The title indicates analysis of tuberculosis underreporting and HIV testing, but the absent abstract prevents judging the letter's methodological content.
This HIV-associated leishmaniasis survey combines serology, PCR, species identification, and risk-factor analysis to measure largely asymptomatic infection.
This study probabilistically links an HCV cohort to death registrations to quantify omitted HCV attribution and interpret mortality trends.
This observational study reports clinical characteristics and outcomes of visceral-leishmaniasis patients with and without HIV using standard comparisons.
This febrile-illness investigation combines ELISA and neutralization testing to uncover chikungunya circulation otherwise confused with dengue.
This study develops and independently assesses sex-specific fetal head-circumference reference charts for surveillance of Zika-associated microcephaly.
This cohort study validates a mobile symptom diary against conventional recording for detecting infectious otitis episodes outside healthcare.
This validation study compares self-reported antiretroviral use with mass-spectrometric drug detection and estimates reporting sensitivity and specificity.
This individual-based simulation tests whether observational adjustment can distinguish biological HIV–HSV-2 interaction from shared sexual-behavior confounding.
This evaluation uses household interviews, medical records, and surveillance data to quantify zoonosis underascertainment, underreporting, and reporting delay.
This vaccine-impact study combines two active-surveillance sources with capture-recapture correction to estimate age- and serotype-specific pneumococcal incidence.
This rabies application uses community questionnaires and spatial mixed-effects models to correct geographically varying livestock-death reporting.
This maternity-based study compares tested syphilis cases with official notifications to estimate prevalence, congenital incidence, transmission, and underreporting.
This national survey describes arthroplasty antibiotic and DAIR protocols but does not validate or correct registry reporting.
This One Health record review integrates human, livestock, and wildlife surveillance to map anthrax hotspots and expose cross-sector underreporting.
This pre-Zika birth-cohort analysis establishes population baselines and definition-specific microcephaly estimates for interpreting infection-associated surveillance.
This systematic review synthesizes comparative evidence on urinary, bacterial-vaginosis, HIV, and STI outcomes after female genital cutting.
This national surveillance summary reports healthcare-associated infection incidence and mortality without correcting the acknowledged underreporting.
This multistage probability survey traces sampled HIV patients lost to follow-up and uses weights to correct treatment mortality.
This HIV-HBV application compares point-of-care urinary alcohol biomarkers with self-report to identify underreporting and its predictors.
This review evaluates depression treatment among people with HIV but reports no infectious or HIV-specific outcome and no methodological transfer route.
This Ebola analysis estimates spatiotemporally varying reporting rates and shows how their correction changes time-varying reproduction-number estimates.
This chikungunya study combines sampled community searches with complete institutional-record review to partition sources of epidemic underreporting.
This HIV-related application uses mass spectrometry to validate contraceptive self-reports and characterize bidirectional exposure misclassification.
This historical maternal-mortality study evaluates certification practices without identifying a concrete transfer of its findings to infectious-disease measurement.
This field report identifies underrecording of maternal hepatitis C status as a barrier to appropriate infant testing.
This study evaluates reported injection-drug use within social networks as a proxy for stigmatized personal exposure in HCV risk studies.
This global tuberculosis analysis adjusts notification data for incomplete age disaggregation and underreporting to estimate adolescent incidence.
This advocacy article argues for congenital-toxoplasmosis screening but supplies insufficient comparative or evaluative method content.
This HIV-cascade comparison probability-matches household and sex-worker samples to reduce occupational-status underreporting before estimating service uptake.
This provider survey describes knowledge and practices for dog-bite and rabies management without a transferable surveillance correction or validation.
This review estimates neuropathic pain across predominantly noninfectious neuropathies and does not provide a concrete infectious-epidemiology method.
This study develops and evaluates a multiplex structural-protein microarray for distinguishing antibody responses to multiple cocirculating alphaviruses.
This population survey compares biomarker-diagnosed with self-reported sexually transmitted infections and identifies differential reporting determinants.
This HCV-treatment review concerns vascular adverse events rather than infection outcomes and gives no concrete infectious-method transfer route.
This active-search study combines microscopy, PCR, logistic regression, and correspondence analysis to characterize spatially patterned mixed-species malaria.
This systematic review synthesizes needlestick prevalence, reporting rates, and reporting barriers relevant to blood-borne infection surveillance.
This hepatitis B study combines surveillance exposures with population hospitalization registries to estimate attributable nosocomial transmission.
This linked-data validation quantifies pathogen-specific undercoding in hospital discharges and incomplete stool sampling among gastroenteritis patients.
This narrative review updates dengue occurrence in Nigeria and recommends better surveillance without comparing or developing surveillance methods.
This study compares three parallel malaria information systems to locate level-specific overreporting, underreporting, and data-integrity failures.
This evaluation assesses an immunization adverse-event system for usefulness, simplicity, acceptability, timeliness, stability, representativeness, cost, and completeness.
This static population model estimates the health and cost-effectiveness effects of maternal pertussis vaccination under unreported-case assumptions.
This case report concerns a noninfectious cutaneous drug reaction and offers no infectious-epidemiology transfer route.
This registry-linked cohort compares confirmed prosthetic-joint infections with insurance claims to measure claim underreporting and its predictors.
This multicentre application uses standardized CDC and consortium definitions to compare device-associated infection and resistance rates across intensive-care units.
This scoping review compares classification, regression, clustering, lexicon, and basic consumer-data methods for transferable foodborne-illness surveillance.
This review describes Nigerian livestock arboviruses and surveillance needs without substantively comparing or prescribing a developed method.
This validation study compares parental vaccination recall with cards and estimates agreement, bias, sensitivity, specificity, and predictors of error.
This review pools state validations of bloodstream-infection reporting accuracy and categorizes recurrent causes of surveillance misclassification.
This prospective comparison quantifies respiratory infections missed by routine coding and testing and evaluates competing influenza-like-illness definitions.
This review describes Venezuelan coccidioidomycosis and proposes ecological surveillance without evaluating a transferable method.
This single feline otitis case identifies a Corynebacterium species but adds no epidemiologic method, correction, or guidance resource.
This study corrects opioid-overdose mortality coding and provides no concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This study maps tuberculosis diagnostic capacity and uses hierarchical models to identify facility and county determinants of guideline-concordant testing.
This broad surgical-quality review does not make infection an analytic outcome or state a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This chikungunya study estimates age-specific excess mortality against historical baselines and adjusts deaths for epidemic underreporting.
This systematic review compares pertussis economic-evaluation strategies and identifies underreporting, herd protection, and coverage assumptions driving results.
This prospective cohort evaluates a structured pharmacovigilance training model by measuring knowledge, report completeness, committees, and reporting increases.
This paper diagnoses structural, sociocultural, conflict-related, and surveillance barriers to HIV measurement and derives lessons for conflict-affected settings.
This comparative study validates a malaria rapid test against microscopy and nested PCR while examining antigen-gene diversity affecting performance.
This fever-surveillance study combines paired molecular and serological diagnostics with capture-recapture estimation of dengue underreporting.
This mechanistic model comparison uses Bayesian fitting and epidemiological coherence checks to evaluate dengue model structures under underreporting.
This Ebola field report integrates surveillance, contact-tracing, laboratory, facility, and expected-mortality data to identify geographic reporting gaps.
This clinical review addresses multifactorial blepharitis management without an infectious-surveillance outcome or concrete epidemiologic transfer route.
This HIV-prevention trial analysis evaluates two dapivirine biomarkers against self-reported vaginal-ring adherence and quantifies age-varying misclassification.
This single tinea nigra case documents an unusual diagnosis but contributes no transferable epidemiologic method or surveillance guidance.
This student survey describes knowledge and attitudes toward disease notification without evaluating reporting performance or a corrective method.
This immunization data-quality audit uses recounted doses, verification factors, and process indices to identify reporting inconsistencies.
This study evaluates complementary skin-test and proliferative T-cell measurements and a membrane-antigen assay for detecting latent tuberculosis.
This nurse questionnaire describes occupational-exposure reporting behavior and associated factors without validating or correcting a surveillance measure.
This review synthesizes geographic, phenotypic, and parasite-species evidence on atypical leishmaniasis with implications for detection and elimination.
This systematic review shows how inconsistent virological-failure and genotyping definitions underreport HIV resistance and recommends standardized thresholds.
This simulation study evaluates critical-slowing-down indicators as underreporting-robust measures of progress toward infectious-disease elimination.
This review summarizes flaviviral ocular manifestations and laboratory pathogenesis without an epidemiologic design or transferable surveillance method.
This review collates published, local, and unpublished Philippine melioidosis cases to characterize burden despite absent routine surveillance.
This dermatology review concerns a noninfectious keratoderma and offers no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This measles study validates reporting completeness by both survey-register matching and comparison of susceptible-population estimates with notifications.
This birth-certificate validation targets cesarean quality adjustment and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This Lyme analysis triangulates cross-border incidence, serology performance, and tick and canine surveillance to estimate human underdetection.
This study uses probabilistic linkage between tuberculosis and AIDS notification systems to estimate underreporting and its service-level predictors.
This infoveillance application compares Google Trends signals with European pertussis cases and deaths to assess outbreak-monitoring performance.
This hepatitis C surveillance assessment estimates timeliness, sensitivity, underreporting, false-reporting, and predictive value across three years.
This nursing article addresses age-related skin care rather than infectious disease and states no concrete transfer route.
This cost-benefit analysis adapts a Salmonella risk assessment to compare five farm-control strategies and propagate reporting uncertainty.
This four-couple HPV cancer case series raises a transmission hypothesis but adds no reusable epidemiologic design or measurement method.
This multisite study compares animal-bite records with national surveillance and models factors associated with rabies-vaccine initiation and completion.
This study compares invasive-bacterial-disease notifications with hospital discharges and imputes unspecified etiologies to assess reporting completeness and trends.
This news report describes Europe's hepatitis challenge without sufficient methodological, diagnostic, correction, or guidance content.
This acute-fever investigation applies pathogen-specific molecular testing to reveal clinically indistinguishable bacterial infections missed by syndromic surveillance.
This STI study uses laboratory testing and a Y-chromosome biomarker to assess sexual-exposure underreporting alongside bacterial-vaginosis risk.
This cholera analysis combines nonparametric and segmented epidemic curves with pair-correlation functions to estimate temporal and spatial transmission structure.
This new graph-based evidence-synthesis framework is tested on simulated and dog-rabies data for multimodal outbreak clustering under underreporting.
This neonatal bacteremia case report discusses treatment but contributes no transferable epidemiologic method or surveillance resource.
This ethical commentary concerns artificial-intelligence suicide prevention and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This ART cohort uses an alcohol biomarker to quantify geographically varying self-report bias and its association with HIV disease stage.
This single-centre case series describes cutaneous mycobacterial presentations and outcomes without a transferable surveillance or measurement contribution.
This HIV study uses hepatitis C serology to detect likely underreported injection drug use and reassess attributed transmission routes.
This retrospective dengue study reports acute-kidney-injury prevalence and standard regression associations without a reusable measurement or correction method.
This multilevel analysis compares parental HPV-vaccination reports with provider records and models individual, household, provider, and state predictors of error.
This clinical letter describes underrecognized Seoul hantavirus characteristics specifically to improve diagnosis and reduce case underreporting.
This study demonstrates how short follow-up and restrictive definitions miss groin-wound infections and argues for uniform reporting standards.
This chikungunya application estimates epidemic excess deaths from historical age-specific mortality and correlates them with monthly incidence.
This sentinel-surveillance report describes French STI trends and patient characteristics without correcting the acknowledged reporting changes.
This blinded record review validates routine healthcare-associated infection surveillance and identifies definition, workload, and staffing causes of error.
This pooled observational analysis reports HCV treatment effectiveness and safety, mentioning underreporting only as a limitation.
This study evaluates milk as a noninvasive alternative specimen for foot-and-mouth-disease virus detection, typing, and targeted surveillance.
This review addresses outcome measurement in elderly asthma without an infectious outcome or stated infectious-disease transfer route.
This systematic review adjusts cutaneous-leishmaniasis incidence for underreporting and models previously omitted depression and scarring burden.
This dietary-questionnaire validation concerns nonalcoholic fatty liver disease and gives no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This syphilis record review audits compulsory notification forms and identifies substantial incompleteness in epidemiological, clinical, and laboratory fields.
This systematic scoping review appraises the organization, implementation, reporting pathways, detection capacity, and fragmentation of Indian dengue surveillance.
This vaccine-safety application combines long-term pharmacovigilance case characterization, dose-based reporting rates, and individual and person-year observed-versus-expected analyses.
This single transplant-recipient HSV case illustrates an atypical presentation but adds no transferable epidemiologic method or formal guidance resource.
This healthcare-worker serosurvey describes hepatitis B prevalence, vaccination, exposures, and injury reporting using standard cross-sectional analyses.
